title: Assertion fault on double LEFT JOIN
description:
drh added on 2013-07-01 16:47:06:
The following script causes an assertion fault:
CREATE TABLE p (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  uid VARCHAR(36),
  t INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE pa (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  a_uid VARCHAR(36)
);

CREATE TABLE a (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  uid VARCHAR(36),
  t INTEGER
);

SELECT DISTINCT *
FROM
  pa
  LEFT JOIN p ON p.uid='1234'
  LEFT JOIN a ON a.uid=pa.a_uid
WHERE
  a.t=p.t
;
This problem was reported on the SQLite mailing list by Jens Miltner.
Bisecting shows that the problem was introduced by check-in
[5f4907e1c6230e3d] that added support for transitive constraints.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE p (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, uid VARCHAR(36), t INTEGER DEFAULT 0);
CREATE TABLE pa (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, a_uid VARCHAR(36) UNIQUE NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE a (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, uid VARCHAR(36), t INTEGER CHECK(t >= 0));
SELECT DISTINCT * FROM pa LEFT JOIN p ON p.uid='1234' LEFT JOIN a ON a.uid=pa.a_uid WHERE a.t IS NULL OR a.t=p.t;
key_bug_features: Assertion fault on double LEFT JOIN with transitive constraints
